message: br-bd-u2g7m add openfs recovery fuzz harness

 {"id":"bd-zmxf3","title":"port ext4 external xattr block sorted-order parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:09:55.582459448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T23:16:50.300417521Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T23:16:50.298358967Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 external xattr parser parity coverage: entry-hash word mutations preserve sorted logical parse result; rch test/fmt/check passed and crate-local clippy is clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zvl5z","title":"Freeze exact FfsError string-variant debug contract","description":"Tighten the remaining substring-only ffs-error debug-format test for string variants to an exact golden contract, then validate through rch.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:47:35.043413450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:53:48.363131060Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:53:48.360789138Z","close_reason":"Froze the remaining substring-only ffs-error string-variant debug test to an exact golden contract and validated with rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-error","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-zzikz","title":"port ext4 inode i_version rename parity","notes":"Kernel ext4 rename bumps ctime/i_version, not i_generation; this bead tracks the actual rename change-cookie contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:27:20.689706867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T13:37:00.444549721Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T13:37:00.441642658Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 rename i_version parity regression and clarified generation preservation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-u2g7m","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-core mounted-image byte-corruption recovery harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-core` open/mount recovery boundary with raw byte corruption as the driver. Build a libFuzzer harness that mutates minimally valid ext4 and btrfs images, then opens them through `OpenFs::from_device(...)` / `OpenFs::open_with_options(...)` while recovery-related knobs vary. The harness should focus on repair/recover paths instead of only parser entry points.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2132-2387` mount/open path
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2755-2929` ext4 crash/orphan recovery
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2204-2235` btrfs tree-log recovery branch

Coverage goals:
- random byte corruption in superblocks, root-tree blocks, orphan links, log roots, and recovery flags
- deterministic classification into `FfsError`, safe replay skip, or successful mount
- no panic, no hang, no unbounded traversal during corrupted-image recovery

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- shared corpus seeded from current clean/dirty ext4 and btrfs recovery fixtures
- crashers minimized and promoted to regressions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","testing","fuzzing","ffs-core","recovery","repair","mount-path"]}
+{"id":"bd-u2g7m","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-core mounted-image byte-corruption recovery harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-core` open/mount recovery boundary with raw byte corruption as the driver. Build a libFuzzer harness that mutates minimally valid ext4 and btrfs images, then opens them through `OpenFs::from_device(...)` / `OpenFs::open_with_options(...)` while recovery-related knobs vary. The harness should focus on repair/recover paths instead of only parser entry points.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2132-2387` mount/open path
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2755-2929` ext4 crash/orphan recovery
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2204-2235` btrfs tree-log recovery branch

Coverage goals:
- random byte corruption in superblocks, root-tree blocks, orphan links, log roots, and recovery flags
- deterministic classification into `FfsError`, safe replay skip, or successful mount
- no panic, no hang, no unbounded traversal during corrupted-image recovery

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- shared corpus seeded from current clean/dirty ext4 and btrfs recovery fixtures
- crashers minimized and promoted to regressions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:18:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","testing","fuzzing","ffs-core","recovery","repair","mount-path"]}
 {"id":"bd-p8c4q","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-core journal-replay recovery harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the journal-driven recovery boundary in `ffs-core`. Build a harness that fuzzes journal bytes plus replay-policy knobs through the real mount/open path so ext4 JBD2 replay, fast-commit evidence, external-journal pairing, orphan cleanup, and MVCC WAL replay interact the same way they do in production open flows.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:3121-3175` ext4 journal replay dispatch
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:3327-3452` fast-commit evidence + verification
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2477-2584` MVCC WAL recovery
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:3045-3119` external journal pairing/probe path

Harness classes to cover:
- internal journal bytes / segment corruption
- external journal UUID + block-size mismatch combinations
- truncated or malformed fast-commit tails
- WAL corruption under `TailPolicy` variants

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- corpus seeded from current JBD2, fast-commit, WAL, and external-journal fixtures
- malformed replay inputs never panic and remain bounded to stable error classes or safe fallback reports","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","testing","fuzzing","ffs-core","journal","recovery","mount-path"]}
 {"id":"bd-w1n6b","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-fuse ioctl argument harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-fuse` ioctl dispatch boundary. Build a fuzz harness around `dispatch_ioctl(...)` / `dispatch_ioctl_for_testing(...)` that mutates `cmd`, `in_data`, `out_size`, `fh`, `caller_pid`, and read-only/writeable mount options while using the existing recorder/test doubles. The goal is to stress the argument parsers and ioctl response encoding without needing a live mount.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1213-1353` FIEMAP parsing/encoding helpers
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1241-1300` move_ext / u32 / flag parsers
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1536-1833` ioctl dispatch for FIEMAP, GET/SETFLAGS, GET/SETVERSION, fscrypt, move_ext, fslabel, and btrfs ioctl cases

Mutation classes:
- short/oversized ioctl buffers
- inconsistent `out_size` vs payload sizes
- garbage command numbers / incompatible flag bits
- malformed fscrypt `_EX`, move_ext, FIEMAP, and fslabel argument layouts

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- harness proves no panic across malformed ioctl argument shapes
- any crashers become regression tests next to the current unit coverage","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","testing","fuzzing","ffs-fuse","ioctl","mount-path"]}
 {"id":"bd-t6k9r","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-fuse path-encoding mount-path harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the raw path/name boundary in `ffs-fuse`. Build a harness that constructs `OsStr`/raw-byte names with `std::os::unix::ffi::OsStrExt`, then drives `lookup`, `mkdir`, `create`, `rename`, `symlink`, `readlink`, and `readdir` through the existing test doubles and mounted-path helper surfaces. The goal is to freeze behavior for non-UTF8 names, separator-like bytes, empty names, overlong names, and path-encoding corner cases before they become mounted-path regressions.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1970-2027` mkdir/rename dispatch
- `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2244-2445` lookup/readdir/symlink entry points
- current mounted-path helpers in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` that already exercise `OsStrExt` and raw-byte name behavior

Mutation classes:
- non-UTF8 path bytes
- embedded slash / NUL-adjacent byte sequences where constructible
- empty, dot, dotdot, PATH_MAX-adjacent, and rename target/source combinations
- readlink/symlink targets that preserve raw bytes across mount-path lookup and readdir

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- corpus seeded from current fscrypt-nokey raw-name and rename/readlink mounted-path cases
- malformed path bytes never panic and always classify to stable errno/error outcomes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","testing","fuzzing","ffs-fuse","path-encoding","mount-path"]}
